title: Brute Force & Password Attacks
description: Systematically trying all possible passwords or using wordlists to gain unauthorized access.
fullExplanation: Brute force attacks try every possible password combination until the correct one is found. Pure brute force is slow — modern attacks use wordlists (dictionary attacks), rules (l33tspeak substitutions), and credential stuffing (reusing leaked passwords). Offline attacks against stolen password hashes are extremely fast — modern GPUs crack billions of hashes per second.

types.name: Pure Brute Force
types.description: Try every combination — aaaa, aaab, aaac... Slow but exhaustive.

types.name: Dictionary Attack
types.description: Try words from a wordlist (rockyou.txt has 14M passwords). Fast and effective.

types.name: Rule-based Attack
types.description: Apply rules to wordlist: Password → P@ssw0rd, password123, PASSWORD. Hashcat rules.

types.name: Credential Stuffing
types.description: Use leaked username:password pairs from other breaches. Works because of password reuse.

types.name: Password Spraying
types.description: Try one common password (Password1!) against many accounts. Avoids lockout.

types.name: Rainbow Table
types.description: Precomputed hash→password lookup table. Defeated by salting.

keyFacts: rockyou.txt: 14 million real passwords from 2009 RockYou breach — still most used wordlist
RTX 4090 cracks MD5 at 164 billion hashes/second
bcrypt/Argon2 designed to be slow — 100 hashes/second on same GPU
Password spraying: 1 attempt per account per 30 min avoids lockout
HaveIBeenPwned: check if your email is in known breaches
Credential stuffing success rate: ~0.1-2% but millions of accounts tried
8-char MD5 password cracked in seconds; bcrypt takes years

defenses.name: Account Lockout
defenses.description: Lock account after N failed attempts — stops online brute force

defenses.name: MFA
defenses.description: Password alone isn't enough — second factor required

defenses.name: Strong Password Policy
defenses.description: Minimum 12 chars, complexity, check against breach databases

defenses.name: Rate Limiting
defenses.description: Limit login attempts per IP per time window

defenses.name: Password Manager
defenses.description: Unique random passwords per site — eliminates credential stuffing

defenses.name: Bcrypt / Argon2
defenses.description: Slow hash functions — make offline cracking impractical
